title: Browser Trust, CORS, CSRF, Cookies, and Security Headers · Contract Lens: Security Header Policy
summary: A browser API reflects origins, combines credentials with wildcard sharing, treats CORS as access control, relies only on SameSite, and emits weak cookie and framing policy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts security header policy increment in browser request and response-sharing policy matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Deploy CSP, frame-ancestors, HSTS at the correct authority, MIME sniffing prevention, referrer policy, permissions policy, and cache controls with browser tests.
Set Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Path, Domain, lifetime, host-only or prefix constraints, partitioning decisions, rotation, and deletion consistently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Security Header Policy · Theory Security Header Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 3690 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to keep CORS response sharing distinct from CSRF mutation authorization; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then lower a byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at its owner. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Deploy CSP, frame-ancestors, HSTS at the correct authority, MIME sniffing prevention, referrer policy, permissions policy, and cache controls with browser tests.
